steps.title: Use both halves of the claim
steps.content: The passage says translation apps have become useful for simple, brief tasks, but they still fail to convey many nuances. Richer communication in business or personal exchanges depends on patterns and gradations of meaning that the apps do not reliably handle.

steps.title: Reject unsupported extremes
steps.content: B invents tourist use and business skepticism. C shifts to ease of use and language coverage, which the passage does not discuss. D overstates the limitation by saying apps are inadequate for most practical purposes, even though the passage says they are useful for perfunctory tasks.

steps.title: Choose the balanced claim
steps.content: The statement that apps have improved but remain insufficient for complex interactions captures both parts of the passage. The answer is A.
